topic: Why Printer Ink Costs More Than Champagne

packaging.title: Why Printer Ink Costs More Than Champagne
altTitles: The Real Reason Printer Ink Is So Expensive
How Printers Are Sold at a Loss on Purpose
packaging.description: A breakdown of the razor-and-blades pricing model behind inkjet printers, why a gallon of ink costs more than fine champagne, and how manufacturers engineer cartridges to keep you locked in.

thumbnail.visualIdea: A tiny ink cartridge on a price tag next to a champagne bottle, the cartridge clearly winning

packaging.hookPromise: Drop for drop, the ink in your printer costs more than vintage champagne, and the printer itself is sold to you at a loss on purpose, so you'll keep refilling at their price.
openLoops: Why would a company sell you the printer for less than it costs to make?
What's actually inside a cartridge that justifies a $2700-per-gallon price?
Why does your printer refuse to print in black when only the color ink runs out?
packaging.payoff: The cartridge contains a microchip that counts pages and locks itself out before the ink is actually gone, meaning you throw away paid-for ink to force a repurchase, the printer is the bait, the ink is the trap.
packaging.whyItWorks: Everyone has cursed at printer ink, and the champagne comparison turns a universal annoyance into a shocking number that demands explanation.
